Specification: King XP Tangless Main Bearing Set
Notes: Verified. PERFORMANCE APPROACH — bypasses OEM select-fit color code system entirely. VW/Audi uses a color-coded select-fit bearing system (Blue/BLA, Red/ROT, Yellow/GLB, Black) where block stamps + crank stamps cross-reference to a specific bearing shell thickness per journal. Forged cranks commonly require Blue (BLA) shells: 07K105561FBLA upper / 07K105591FBLA lower, ~$76.74/shell × 12 shells = ~$920 full OEM set vs $195.99 King XP. King XP tri-metal standard clearance covers the forged crank range — Euromotive verifies clearance with Plastigage at assembly. Simpler and cheaper. OEM Blue shells currently in stock at UroTuning (https://www.urotuning.com/products/main-bearing-upper-vw-audi-2-5l-rabbit-jetta-golf-beetle-passat-07k105561fbla) — the 'out of stock in US' issue documented in at least one community build (Bumblebee/Nikita) was likely a 2024-era availability gap with superseded PN; current F-revision is available domestically. Stick with King XP.

Specification: TTRS / RS3 / RSQ3 Oil Spray Jets — OEM part# 06J103154B (qty 5, one per cylinder)
Notes: PART NUMBER: 06J103154B (post-Aug 2010 TTRS spec; supersedes 06F103154A). 5 jets per engine. Two design differences vs stock BGP NA jets: (1) Banjo bolt — 4 oil feed holes vs 2 on BGP; holes are slightly smaller each but double the total flow. Also features refined relief valve location. (2) Jet tip — larger opening ('thicker' shot vs stock 'mist'); more tapered exit = higher exit pressure (garden-hose-thumb effect). WHY: Turbo blowby gas increases crankcase backpressure vs NA. Stock BGP squirters were sized for NA pressure levels — under turbo boost, squirter output may not fully overcome the higher backpressure to cool the piston crown effectively. TTRS jets are factory-engineered for exactly this application. INSTALLATION: Same thread as BGP block — hand thread in, torque to 27 ft-lbs (per VW erwin service spec). Zero additional machine work. Install during longblock assembly. TRADE-OFF: Marginal oil pressure drop from higher squirter flow. Mitigated by LPS oil pump mod already in this build. Community data: Kyle Brixey ran OEM squirters at 800hp (64mm turbo) with no issues; Robert Helble notes 'road course, time attack, hill climb — you may want to pull heat away from the piston' — drag builds less critical. This is a road course + drift build; upgrade is warranted and negligible in cost. ADDITIONAL CONFIRMED PART NUMBERS: 06J103154AA (EA888 Gen1 — confirmed bolt-in, physically identical to CEPA/TTRS spec per Carson Heter). Alt approach: EA888 Gen3 squirter tips + OEM 07K banjo bolt also confirmed to work (Julio César Mireles). NOTE: 06J103154AA may be flagged as 'Gen2 TSI' by part lookup — this is the engine family it's catalogued under, not an indication it won't fit; community confirms fitment. BEST SOURCE: VW/Audi dealer with heavy OEM discounts — Genuine Audi Parts or Miramar Audi (CA) recommended. Ref: CaddyFab Big Turbo 07K Build Thread (VW Vortex) + FB thread 1: https://www.facebook.com/groups/07kswap/permalink/3608542169312397/ + FB thread 2: https://www.facebook.com/groups/07kswap/permalink/3608094966023784/

Specification: 07K109120A - Lower Timing Chain
07K109231A - Upper Timing Chain
07K115121F - Oil Pump Drive Sprocket
07K109077E - Timing Chain Idler Sprocket
Op1: M14x2 tap + button head bolts + Resbond 907TS Blue. Op2: EGR flow port blanking plates + Permatex Ultra Copper
Notes: MANDATORY for any boosted 07K — TWO SEPARATE OPERATIONS (confirmed @wingman703). Do on stand before dropoff or on sealed longblock. OP1 (FREEZE PLUG HOLES front+rear): Remove OEM pressed-in plugs. Tap M14x2 — no drilling needed. Install M14x2 button head socket cap bolts — dome head only; rear zero clearance for standard hex. Seal threads Resbond 907TS Blue (+2100F) NOT Loctite NOT RTV. Peen/stake bore edge. Parts: tap ~$20 (McMaster or local) + bolts x2 ~$5-10 (McMaster 92949A533) + Resbond 907TS Blue ~$35 (Amazon B0089YLDNE). OP2 (EGR FLOW PORT BLANKING plates front+rear): Circular EGR flow ports on block face (rubber-gasket seats ~2 mounting bolts each) are SEPARATE from freeze plug holes. Apply Permatex Ultra Copper high-temp RTV around port perimeter. Bolt flat blanking plates over each port front and rear. Plates: source/fabricate from 1/8 steel or 6061 aluminum to match EGR port bolt pattern. Permatex Ultra Copper ~$12. PITFALL: Resbond for bolt threads ONLY. Blanking plates use Permatex Ultra Copper RTV for face seal — do not use Resbond on face seal.

Specification: Pierburg CWA400 (PWM version) — MaxxECU RACE PWM GPO control. Confirmed: @wingman703 (CWA400 controlled by the Maxx ECU)
Notes: CONFIRMED BY @wingman703: CWA400 controlled by MaxxECU (not Davies Craig EWP150). Brushless centrifugal OEM pump (BMW/VW) — 150 LPM @ 0.85 bar (vs 40 LPM EWP150). No separate pump controller — MaxxECU GPO is sole PWM driver. VERSION CRITICAL: must source PWM version ONLY. PWM part#s: Pierburg 7.07223.10.0 / BMW 11515A05704 / 11517563659 / 11517568594. LIN version (do NOT use): Pierburg 7.03665.66.0 / BMW 11517604027 (post-March 2024 production — not PWM-controllable). SOURCING: New ~$720 Dedicated Motorsports; OEM BMW pull eBay $50-150 used — verify PWM part# before buying. MAXXECU CONFIG: PWM GPO at 680 Hz. Duty cycle map: 20% at 60C / 55% at 85C / 97% at 105C. Duty 13-85% = controlled speed; 86-97% = full speed. Wake pulse: MaxxECU must output >=3ms uninterrupted high at ignition-on before CLT map. POWER WIRING: 40A relay (Bosch 0 332 002 150) + 10 AWG feed/ground (35.5A max draw). CONNECTOR: Kostal 2+2 (4-pin) — Pin 1=PWM signal (22 AWG), Pin 2=BSD unused, Pin 3=+12V (10 AWG), Pin 4=GND (10 AWG). POST-SHUTDOWN: MaxxECU power hold relay required — keeps ECU alive to command pump until CLT <70C. BRING 07K121011B to Euromotive — remove impeller only, housing stays as coolant manifold. @wingman703 does full pump delete (removes housing + welds off rear port + deletes heater core — Miata track/no cabin heat) — this build retains housing intentionally for cabin heat (street car). Wiring ref: https://github.com/wesleyxcooper/e36-wiring/blob/main/ewp-wiring-reference.md. 944 COMMUNITY VALIDATION (Rennlist 07K swap megathread): Longitudinal 07K builders across 944/968 swaps independently converged on the same EWP approach — block-off plate at pump housing bolt location + electric pump. 'The electric pump will double as an after run pump, so that simplifies the plumbing. I like the idea of only one level of belts — a 2-row idler just to run the stock water pump and alternator seems like extra work.' (Cjar, Rennlist p107). Coolant routing for longitudinal 07K: 5/8 + 3/4 in hose + formed hoses + GM heater valve — documented by alliedcam1 (YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sCYZoCULSXo). Key Rennlist refs: p107 water pump discussion https://rennlist.com/forums/944-turbo-and-turbo-s-forum/803341-vw-audi-07k-2-5l-20v-i5-swap-thread-107.html | p138 coolant line guide https://rennlist.com/forums/944-turbo-and-turbo-s-forum/803341-vw-audi-07k-2-5l-20v-i5-swap-thread-138.html

Specification: alliedcam1 944 07K coolant hose routing (longitudinal orientation — direct reference for E36 build)
Notes: REFERENCE: alliedcam1 'Porsche 944 07k Swap Coolant Line Overview' (Oct 2024). Diagram: https://photos.app.goo.gl/dAAYu7NJUUTBheyb8. Rennlist p138 coolant guide: https://rennlist.com/forums/944-turbo-and-turbo-s-forum/803341-vw-audi-07k-2-5l-20v-i5-swap-thread-138.html. Contact: Spencer (sp.sansouci@gmail.com). PARTS USED (944 chassis — adapt routing at install for E36): Heater Valve GM 12380318 (4-port inline heater valve — directly portable to E36 build); Dorman Help! 56152 Heater Fittings (https://www.amazon.com/Dorman-Help-56152-Heater-Fittings/dp/B000COCWEQ); Dayco 72193 (radiator lower hose); Dayco 72365 (overflow/reservoir line); Gates 22503 (radiator hose); Gates 20416 (radiator hose); HPS Silicone 180-Degree Elbow HTSEC180-075-BLK (3/4 in silicone elbow); URO 032121142 C-Clip Retainer (cooling hose flange clip); 5/8 in coolant hose x 10 ft; 3/4 in coolant hose x 10 ft; 90-degree formed hose 3/4 in; 180-degree formed hose 5/8 in; 90-degree formed hose 5/8 in; 5/16 in vent line to reservoir; Coolant Hose Splice Kit; stock 944 lower coolant line (reused). NOTE: exact hose part numbers are 944-chassis-specific and will not transfer directly to E36. Heater valve GM 12380318 + general approach (formed hoses + inline valve + 5/8+3/4 bulk hose) ARE portable. Size the E36 routing at Phase 3 install.

Specification: Custom shorter belt — alternator + passive water pump pulley (route TBD at Phase 3 install)
Notes: Water pump IS belt-driven (accessory belt not timing chain). With impeller removed and housing in place the water pump PULLEY stays in the belt path as a passive free-spinning routing point. Active belt-driven load = alternator only. A/C compressor (electric) and PS pump (column EPS) are fully deleted. Stock 07K accessory belt path (AC + PS + water pump + alternator) is no longer valid — need a custom shorter belt routing alternator + water pump pulley + idler. Closest reference product: Eurowise 07K AC Delete Kit EW07ACDK $425 (shop.eurowise.com/products/07k-ac-delete-kit) — billet dual-belt alternator pulley + idler + both belts. Designed for MK-platform swaps (MK1 MK4-MK6) not E36 longitudinal — may not drop in directly but best reference for the belt solution concept. ACTION: at Phase 3 install confirm pulley positions in longitudinal E36 orientation and source custom belt. Flag to Euromotive at Phase 2 dropoff. Gates serpentine belt selector for belt length once pulley layout confirmed. 944 COMMUNITY (Rennlist p107): 'With no power steering
column10: and water pump it looks like the only thing left that's belt driven is the alternator' — confirms alternator-only belt path is the standard outcome of the EWP delete in any longitudinal 07K swap. Full EWP delete (no housing
column11: no pulley) = even simpler; this build retains housing + pulley as passive routing point to preserve street belt convention.
